title: phlox viscida
intro: Phlox viscida, also known as wood phlox or woodland phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phlox viscida include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine.
